title: Idempotency, Inbox Transactions, and Effects · Execution Trace: Idempotency Fingerprint
summary: A Go 1.26 service team must validate stable identity and fingerprint, compose inbox and domain mutation, and preserve external-effect ambiguity; reviewers reject hidden goroutine ownership, unbounded work, stale channel state, and claims that stop at a nil client error. steps.title: RabbitMQ and Go Predict Idempotency Fingerprint · Theory Idempotency Fingerprint
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go messaging team handles case 9328 during race-enabled integration test with duplicate delivery. Build deterministic Go evidence to reject fingerprint conflicts, suppress completed duplicates, and preserve external ambiguity; keep message identity out of metric labels while preserving causal traces; then add a subscriber responsibility without changing publisher intent.
